evals.prompt: Triage INC-4604: customer applied discount code SPRING15, UI showed -$12.30 applied, but was charged full $82.00. Reproduced in incognito. Order #ORD-20271.
evals.expected_output: Priority superimportant. TLDR explains silent discount failure. Mentions potential systemic impact. Data check section. flagOrderForReview called.

assertions.name: data_check_section
assertions.description: Output contains a Data check section referencing the DB lookup

assertions.name: systemic_impact_mentioned
assertions.description: Output notes this could affect more than just this one customer

evals.prompt: Triage INC-4612: customer sees an order in their account they didn't place — shipped to an unknown Nevada address, billed to an unrecognized card. Order #ORD-19612.
evals.expected_output: Priority superimportant. TLDR identifies data integrity / account crossover issue. Notes two affected parties. flagOrderForReview called.

assertions.name: data_check_section
assertions.description: Output contains a Data check section

assertions.name: two_customers_noted
assertions.description: Output notes that two customers are likely affected (the reporter and the real order owner)

evals.prompt: Triage INC-4571: customer bought a gift shipped to sister, sister wants to exchange color. Asks if recipient can initiate exchange and whether advance exchange is possible.
evals.expected_output: Priority low or medium (no billing error, no data issue). TLDR is concise. Next step routes to support team, no engineering escalation.

assertions.name: correct_priority_low_or_medium
assertions.description: Output assigns priority low or medium (not superimportant or ignore)

assertions.name: no_flag_called
assertions.description: Output does NOT mention flagging or escalating to engineering as urgent

assertions.name: next_step_routes_to_support
assertions.description: Next step routes to customer support or fulfillment, not engineering
